description: Catalogue of deserialization primitives that constitute RCE when fed untrusted bytes, with per-language safe alternatives and references to historical CVEs they have appeared in.

safer: XStream with XStream.setupDefaultSecurity + addPermission(NoTypePermission.NONE) + addPermission(new ExplicitTypePermission(allowList))
Use Protobuf / Avro with strict schemas, no polymorphism
json.loads with a Pydantic / dataclasses_json model and explicit allowlist of fields
torch.load(path, weights_only=True) — required for any downloaded checkpoint
msgpack with strict_types=True and a model layer on top
Use protobuf-net or MessagePack-CSharp with explicit type maps

sinks: Phar metadata (file_exists / file_get_contents on phar:// URI)
safer: json_decode($input, true, $depth, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR) + an explicit schema validator
If unserialize is unavoidable, pass `allowed_classes => false` (PHP 7.0+) or an explicit allowlist array
Use Rails encrypted cookies (cookies.encrypted) not Marshal-signed ones

sinks: eval / Function constructor on JSON-like strings
safer: JSON.parse with a reviver that rejects non-data types
Zod / io-ts schemas applied immediately after JSON.parse

design_principles.rule: Prefer non-polymorphic schema-validated formats (JSON+Schema, Protobuf) over native polymorphic serializers
If serialized data crosses a trust boundary, HMAC it with a fresh secret and verify before deserializing
When polymorphism is required, enforce a strict allowlist of permitted types at the framework level
pickle / Marshal / BinaryFormatter / ObjectInputStream are RCE primitives — never feed them untrusted bytes
PyYAML SafeLoader, SnakeYAML SafeConstructor, Psych safe_load — explicit safe variants only
